channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Cautious optimism in Lebanon as direct talks with Israel progress
item.description: Lebanese officials are hoping that two-day ambassador-level negotiations in the US will yield a new ceasefire deal.

item.title: Iran’s FM urges BRICS states to condemn US-Israeli aggression
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi, spoke at a gathering of foreign ministers in New Delhi on Thursday.

item.title: Palestinian President Abbas pledges elections, reform at Fatah conference
item.description: Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas has been unanimously re-elected leader of the Fatah movement.

item.title: Mbappe and Dembele head up star-studded France World Cup squad
item.description: Kylian Mbappe will spearhead France at the World Cup as national team coach Didier Deschamps announces his 26-man squad.

item.title: Iran, Taiwan, and trade: Trump’s high‑stakes return to Beijing
item.description: As Trump meets Xi in Beijing for the first time in nine years, can trade war, Taiwan and Iran tensions be contained?

item.title: US Air Force rescues 11 after plane crashes into waters off Florida coast
item.description: US Air Force footage showed the rescue of 11 people after a private plane crashed into the Atlantic Ocean

item.title: What does the US hope to achieve from Lebanon-Israel talks?
item.description: As Israeli attacks on Lebanon continue, Hezbollah opposes ceasefire talks in Washington.

item.title: Iran expands tiered internet access amid continued online blackout
item.description: Iranians have had limited internet access during the war, with the government now looking at tiered services.

item.title: Israeli ultranationalists chant racist slogans during Jerusalem Day march
item.description: Annual Jerusalem Day march fuels tensions with shouted slogans and violence against Palestinian residents.

item.title: FIFA warned ‘gruelling heat’ could impact a quarter of World Cup games
item.description: Scientists warn football's governing body that global warming has increased extreme heat risk since 1994 US World Cup.

item.title: Israel’s Ben Gvir storms Al-Aqsa during Jerusalem Day march
item.description: Itamar Ben Gvir stormed the Al-Aqsa compound under heavy military protection during Jerusalem Day.

item.title: Jerome Powell: Steering the US Fed through COVID-19 and political pressures
item.description: Trump’s aggressive push for interest rate cuts shaped Powell’s Fed legacy during his tumultuous leadership.

item.title: Trump administration pledges $1.8bn in additional humanitarian aid to UN
item.description: The extra funds come on top of $2bn announced in December but fall short of past commitments from the US.

item.title: Acute hunger grips nearly 20 million people in war-battered Sudan, says IPC
item.description: A lack of access to food and health facilities will lead to death for many, warns the Norwegian Refugee Council.

item.title: Cuba plunged deeper into fuel crisis amid 22-hour blackouts
item.description: Cuba faced nationwide disruption after officials say the country had completely run out of diesel and fuel oil.

item.title: ‘May your village burn’: Israeli Flag March returns to East Jerusalem
item.description: Far-right Israelis attack Palestinians during the Flag March, intensifying violence and racism in Jerusalem's Old City.

item.title: The Palestinian game fighting to exist
item.description: A developer from the occupied West Bank is turning a 75-year-old Palestinian folk tale into a video game.

item.title: Iran urges BRICS nations to condemn US-Israeli war aggression
item.description: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi also accused the UAE of direct involvement in military operations against Iran.

item.title: Iraq’s parliament approves new Ali al-Zaidi government
item.description: PM sworn in and 14 ​ministers approved ⁠in new government though parliament fails to reach consensus on several posts.

item.title: ‘Absolutely no fuel’: Cuba hit by blackouts, protests amid power outages
item.description: Large parts of eastern Cuba were plunged into darkness in the latest round of nationwide electricity shutdowns.

item.title: Yemen gov’t, Houthis to release more than 1,600 POWs in ‘largest’ swap
item.description: The UN-backed agreement will see the ICRC facilitate the exchange of hundreds of detainees from both sides.

item.title: Lamine Yamal divides opinion with Palestinian flag gesture
item.description: A mural of Barcelona player Lamine Yamal was painted in Gaza after the teenager waved a Palestinian flag.

item.title: Israel’s Netanyahu says suing New York Times over Palestinian rape article
item.description: Israel launches 'defamation' suit after Nicholas Kristof article detailing alleged sexual violence by Israeli forces.

item.title: Latvian PM resigns over handling of stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: Latvia’s Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned following criticism of her government’s handling of stray drones

item.title: No Exit From El Fasher
item.description: Fault Lines and Lighthouse investigate the siege of Sudan’s el-Fasher, where civilians were trapped, starved and killed.
